parsed_review.summary: CP00-478 is a descriptor-only analytics-core pack (RP15.P09.M05.S18-RP15.P09.M06.S07, risk A, 10 units) covering the M05 Permission And Audit Binding tail (3 rows) and M06 Synthetic Fixture Set head (7 rows). Pack identity, range, chain continuity (CP477->CP478->CP479), deliverable distribution {implementation:4, claude_review:2, security_audit:2, test:1, ui:1}, and micro-title distribution {Permission And Audit Binding:3, Synthetic Fixture Set:7} agree across manifest, review-baseline, registry binding (registry.js:426), contract current_pack (analytics-core-contract.json:16/27273/28037), and the live closeout-pack-plan.json (line 409). All 7 required public exports exist (service.js:3210/3243; validators.js:4534/4569/4625/4646/4669) and are surfaced via index.js `export *`; freezeCp478Result/freezeCp478Validation stamp comprehensive no-write/no-runtime/no-leak attestations, and per-row guards (descriptor_only=true, runtime_execution=false, customer_safe_errors_only=true, raw_payload_included=false) are enforced in both the service output and the materialized contract projection. No fs/network/child_process/DB operations exist in service.js or validators.js. The validator script (validate-rp15-analytics-core-contract.mjs) deep-equality-checks contract vs binding/requirements/no_write_attestation and asserts coverage.valid, slice.valid, authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval=false, hermes.production_ready_candidate=true, claude.read_only=true, and handoff to CP00-479/RP15.P09.M06.S08; model.test.js mirrors these invariants. command-evidence.json records all 27 gate commands at exit 0. Authority boundaries hold: production_ready=false (correctly not over-claiming runtime readiness for a descriptor layer), Claude is read-only and not final approver, enterprise trust is not claimed from local validation, and Hermes is used only for deterministic descriptor evidence packets (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime. Review-hardening preflight is valid JSON (exit 0, 1444 bytes, well-formed) and not a forbidden-evidence pattern; allowed tools limited to Read/Grep/Glob with forbidden_tools_present=false. Post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are correctly absent at this read_only_claude_run_raw_capture stage. One non-blocking P3: changed-file-scope.json double-classifies two untracked paths. Note: as the read-only reviewer I cannot re-execute the gate commands; recorded exit-0 evidence is corroborated by verifying the underlying code/contract/test structures those commands assert. Final approval and the enterprise trust claim remain outside this receipt and require human sign-off.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json double-classifies two untracked paths as both implementation and unrelated
findings.details: docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md and docs/closeout-packs/cp00-478/ appear simultaneously in untracked_implementation_files (lines 130-133) and in unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 135-145). This is internally contradictory generated metadata: the cp00-478/ directory is in fact this pack's evidence directory (it holds the manifest.json and command-evidence.json under review), so labeling it 'unrelated' is inaccurate. Impact is benign: neither path is a required_pack_artifact, active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty (nothing in-scope was dropped), and commit governance is driven by manifest.json whose worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved correctly excludes both paths. No effect on production_ready determination, no-write/no-real-data boundaries, or closeout integrity.
findings.recommendation: In the changed-file-scope generator, make the classifications mutually exclusive: keep the pack's own evidence directory (docs/closeout-packs/cp00-478/) and pack-related planning docs out of unrelated_dirty_files_preserved so the metadata matches manifest.json governance. Does not block CP00-478 closeout.
